intro: Lichtensteinia exaltata, also known as the 'Australian Lichen Tree', is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its unique ability to form symbiotic relationships with fungi and algae on its leaves. Traditionally, Lichtensteinia exaltata has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Lichtensteinia exaltata is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications for its wound-healing and skin-soothing properties.
